output.code:
# Let's read the uploaded files
import os, json, re, textwrap, unicodedata, sys, pathlib, itertools

paths = ["/mnt/data/text_to_analyse.txt", "/mnt/data/file-SCRop4gtiqBmak49CLWbk5-Q002.txt"]
existing = [p for p in paths if os.path.exists(p)]
existing

import io, codecs, json, os, re, sys, unicodedata
path = "/mnt/data/file-SCRop4gtiqBmak49CLWbk5-Q002.txt"
with open(path, "rb") as f:
    raw = f.read()
# Try to decode detecting encoding
for enc in ["utf-8-sig", "utf-8", "cp1256", "iso-8859-6", "arabic", "windows-1256"]:
    try:
        text = raw.decode(enc)
        chosen_enc = enc
        break
    except:
        continue
len(text), chosen_enc, text[:3000]

text = text
# Let's list indices of all double quote characters " (U+0022)
quote_indices = [i for i, ch in enumerate(text) if ch == '"']
len(quote_indices), quote_indices[:10], quote_indices[-10:]

# Show context around quotes
contexts = []
for idx in quote_indices:
    start = max(0, idx-40)
    end = min(len(text), idx+40)
    contexts.append((idx, text[start:end]))
contexts[:15]

# Let's compute the quote spans for the first 4 pairs and the last unmatched
pairs = []
it = iter(quote_indices)
qu = quote_indices

# Pair quotes sequentially; for unmatched last, handle separately
pairs = [(qu[i], qu[i+1]) for i in range(0, 8, 2)]  # first four pairs
pairs

# Find the index of next newline after 1072 or end-of-string
start_unmatched = 1072
nl_idx = text.find("\n", start_unmatched+1)
start_unmatched, nl_idx, text[start_unmatched-20:start_unmatched+50]

queries: Extract the list of citations from the text based on the schema
وَإِذْ قَالَ لُقْمَانُ لِابْنِهِ وَهُوَ يَعِظُهُ يَا بُنَيَّ لَا تُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ
وَلَقَدْ آَتَيْنَا لُقْمَانَ الْحِكْمَةَ أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ وَمَنْ يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِ
وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنْسَانَ بِوَالِدَيْهِ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ وَهْنًا عَلَى وَهْنٍ
وَإِن جَاهَدَاكَ عَلَى أَن تُشْرِكَ بِي مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ

output.code:
# Compute spans for the first four citations based on quotes
pairs = [(140,257),(319,474),(533,691),(764,996)]
spans = []
for open_i, close_i in pairs:
    span_start = open_i + 1
    span_end = close_i
    span_text = text[span_start:span_end]
    spans.append((span_start, span_end, span_text))
    
# Unmatched last: find newline index 1228
last_open = 1072
last_end = text.find("\n", last_open+1)
if last_end == -1:
    last_end = len(text)
span_start = last_open + 1
span_end = last_end
last_span_text = text[span_start:span_end]
spans.append((span_start, span_end, last_span_text))

spans

last_span_start, last_span_end, last_span_text = spans[-1]
last_span_text, len(last_span_text)

# Find indices of the piece
sep = " (١٨) "
idx_sep = last_span_text.find(sep)
idx_waqsid = last_span_text.find("وَاقْصِدْ")
idx_sep, idx_waqsid, last_span_text[idx_sep-20: idx_sep+20], last_span_text[idx_waqsid-10: idx_waqsid+30]

spans_dict = {}
# 1: 31:13 from 141 to 257
spans_dict["31:13"] = spans[0]

# 2: 31:12 from 320 to 474
spans_dict["31:12"] = spans[1]

# 3: 31:14 from 534 to 691
spans_dict["31:14"] = spans[2]

# 4: 31:15 from 765 to 996
spans_dict["31:15"] = spans[3]

# 5a: 31:18 within last span
ls_start, ls_end, ls_text = spans[4]
idx_sep = ls_text.find(" (١٨) ")
idx_waqsid = ls_text.find("وَاقْصِدْ")
span_31_18_start = ls_start
span_31_18_end = ls_start + (idx_sep if idx_sep!=-1 else len(ls_text))
span_31_18_text = text[span_31_18_start:span_31_18_end]
spans_dict["31:18"] = (span_31_18_start, span_31_18_end, span_31_18_text)

# 5b: 31:19 start at idx_waqsid to end-of-line
span_31_19_start = ls_start + (idx_waqsid if idx_waqsid!=-1 else len(ls_text))
span_31_19_end = ls_end
span_31_19_text = text[span_31_19_start:span_31_19_end]
spans_dict["31:19"] = (span_31_19_start, span_31_19_end, span_31_19_text)

spans_dict

# Build the citations list programmatically (ensuring exact span_text and indices)
citations = []

def add_aya(key, sura_name, sura_number, aya_number, valid, corrected_text):
    (start, end, st) = spans_dict[key]
    entry = {
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": st,
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": valid,
        "aya_reference": {
            "sura_name": sura_name,
            "sura_number": sura_number,
            "aya_number": aya_number
        },
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": corrected_text,
        "completely_invalid": False if valid else False  # For invalid but not completely invalid (partial)
    }
    citations.append(entry)

# Put corrected_text from file search snippet for the canonical texts:
# 31:13
corr_31_13 = "وَإِذْ قَالَ لُقْمَانُ لِابْنِهِ وَهُوَ يَعِظُهُ يَا بُنَيَّ لَا تُشْرِكْ بِاللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ"
# 31:12
corr_31_12 = "وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنَا لُقْمَانَ الْحِكْمَةَ أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِلَّهِ ۚ وَمَنْ يَشْكُرْ فَإِنَّمَا يَشْكُرُ لِنَفْسِهِ ۖ وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَنِيٌّ حَمِيدٌ"
# 31:14
corr_31_14 = "وَوَصَّيْنَا الْإِنْسَانَ بِوَالِدَيْهِ حَمَلَتْهُ أُمُّهُ وَهْنًا عَلَىٰ وَهْنٍ وَفِصَالُهُ فِي عَامَيْنِ أَنِ اشْكُرْ لِي وَلِوَالِدَيْكَ إِلَيَّ الْمَصِيرُ"
# 31:15
corr_31_15 = "وَإِنْ جَاهَدَاكَ عَلَىٰ أَنْ تُشْرِكَ بِي مَا لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ فَلَا تُطِعْهُمَا ۖ وَصَاحِبْهُمَا فِي الدُّنْيَا مَعْرُوفًا ۖ وَاتَّبِعْ سَبِيلَ مَنْ أَنَابَ إِلَيَّ ۚ ثُمَّ إِلَيَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَأُنَبِّئُكُمْ بِمَا كُنْتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ"
# 31:18
corr_31_18 = "وَلَا تُصَعِّرْ خَدَّكَ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَمْشِ فِي الْأَرْضِ مَرَحًا ۖ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ كُلَّ مُخْتَالٍ فَخُورٍ"
# 31:19
corr_31_19 = "وَاقْصِدْ فِي مَشْيِكَ وَاغْضُضْ مِنْ صَوْتِكَ ۚ إِنَّ أَنْكَرَ الْأَصْوَاتِ لَصَوْتُ الْحَمِيرِ"

# Add citations (with is_valid flags)
add_aya("31:13", "لقمان", 31, 13, True, corr_31_13)
add_aya("31:12", "لقمان", 31, 12, True, corr_31_12)
add_aya("31:14", "لقمان", 31, 14, True, corr_31_14)
add_aya("31:15", "لقمان", 31, 15, True, corr_31_15)
add_aya("31:18", "لقمان", 31, 18, True, corr_31_18)
# For 31:19, it's partial; is_valid False
start_31_19, end_31_19, st_31_19 = spans_dict["31:19"]
citations.append({
    "span_start": start_31_19,
    "span_end": end_31_19,
    "span_text": st_31_19,
    "citation_type": "Aya",
    "is_valid": False,
    "aya_reference": {
        "sura_name": "لقمان",
        "sura_number": 31,
        "aya_number": 19
    },
    "hadeeth_reference": {},
    "corrected_text": corr_31_19,
    "completely_invalid": False
})

result_json = {"citations": citations}
# Print JSON for manual view
json.dumps(result_json, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)[:1000]

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
